article.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 30 points and seven assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder dominated the short-handed Golden State Warriors for a 131-94 win Friday night.
article.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 30 points as Thunder beat short-handed Warriors 131-94

images.caption: Oklahoma City Thunder vs. Golden State Warriors: Game Highlights
images.name: Oklahoma City Thunder vs. Golden State Warriors: Game Highlights

keywords: Oklahoma City Thunder @ Golden State Warriors
article.linkText: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 30 points as Thunder beat short-handed Warriors 131-94
article.story: SAN FRANCISCO -- — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 30 points and seven assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder dominated the short-handed Golden State Warriors for a 131-94 win Friday night. Stephen Curry sat out because of a left ankle sprain and backcourt mate Jimmy Butler became ill earlier in the day. Draymond Green rested, while Jonathan Kuminga missed the game with back soreness. That left Steve Kerr's starting lineup as Brandin Podziemski, Moses Moody, Will Richard, Gui Santos and Quinten Post. Kerr was optimistic Curry would play Saturday against the Jazz. Gilgeous-Alexander shot 10 for 20 with three 3-pointers and converted all seven of his free throws for his seventh game in the last nine with 30 or more points — and now three straight. He had 38 points on 13-for-21 shooting with five 3-pointers in the last matchup against the Warriors. Chet Holmgren contributed 15 points and a season-best 15 rebounds — two off his career high — as Oklahoma City (30-5) won a fourth straight following its first two-game skid of the season. Richard had 13 points and five rebounds, Al Horford and Moody also scored 13 while Pat Spencer dished out 11 assists against the defending champion Thunder, who had lost their previous two road games but also won 124-112 at Golden State exactly a month earlier on Dec. 2. Richard's 3-pointer with 7:19 left in the second quarter got Golden State within 38-36 before Oklahoma City answered with a 19-0 run to go ahead 64-45 at halftime. The Warriors had won two straight, five of six and three in a row at home, where they are in a stretch with 10 of 11 at Chase Center. Up next Thunder: Visit Phoenix on Sunday. Warriors: Host Utah on Saturday night to complete a back-to-back. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/NBA

videos.description: Chet Holmgren throws a lead pass to a cutting Isaiah Joe who dunks it.
videos.headline: Chet feeds Isaiah Joe for a nice Thunder slam

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Chet feeds Isaiah Joe for a nice Thunder slam) 2026/01/03 ESHEET

videos.description: Lugentz Dort gets a 3-pointer to fall for the Thunder before the horn sounds.
videos.headline: Lu Dort buries a rainbow 3 before the halftime buzzer

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Lu Dort buries a rainbow 3 before the halftime buzzer) 2026/01/03 ESHEET

videos.description: Aaron Wiggins gets in transition and lobs it to Jalen Williams for a Thunder slam.
videos.headline: Aaron Wiggins leaves it for a J-Dub throwdown

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Aaron Wiggins leaves it for a J-Dub throwdown) 2026/01/03 ESHEET

videos.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander makes a beautiful to the basket and drops it in for a Thunder bucket.
videos.headline: SGA swiftly lays it in for the Thunder

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (SGA swiftly lays it in for the Thunder) 2026/01/03 ESHEET

videos.description: Jalen Williams gets to the basket with a full head of steam to get the Thunder score.
videos.headline: J-Dub crosses in the lane for the OKC hoop

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (J-Dub crosses in the lane for the OKC hoop) 2026/01/03 ESHEET

videos.description: Pat Spencer knocks down a 3-pointer for the Warriors and gives himself a shot to the face.
videos.headline: Pat Spencer punches himself after draining 3

tracking.trackingName: NBA_One-Play (Pat Spencer punches himself after draining 3) 2026/01/03 ESHEET
